sections.paragraph: (“sand made warm by the heat of the sun”), Arab. عَكَّةُ from the root עָכַךְ pr.n. Accho, a maritime city in the tribe of Asher, Jud. 1:31 (and perhaps Mic 1:10 where בכו seems to be for בעכו); called on the Phœnico-Grecian coins עכ, read עַכֹּ (see Mionnet, Descr. des Medailles, tab. 21. Eckhel, Doctr. Numm. iii. 423 [See Ges. Monum. Phœnic. p. 269]), Greek Ἄκη (Strabo, xvi. 2, § 25); more commonly called Ptolemais; called in the time of the crusades عَكَّةُ, now St. Jeand’ Acre. See Relandi Palæstina, p. 534-42.
